engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Megane Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Megane in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Megane K9K engine - diesel 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo failure - charters fail around 80k miles causing severe loss of power.
Injector wear - piezo injectors degrade causing rough idle and white smoke.

cards.altText: Renault Megane H5F engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - tensioner failure causes chain slap and catastrophic engine damage.
Excessive oil consumption - piston ring design flaw leads to low oil warnings.

cards.altText: Renault Megane F9Q engine - diesel 1.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - belt snap causes immediate valve and piston destruction.
Swirl flap issues - plastic linkage breaks, dropping debris into the cylinders.

engineGuide.h2: Renault Megane Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Renault Megane across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: The backbone of the Megane diesel range, offering exceptional economy but plagued by turbo and injector issues in early generations.
commonFailures: Turbocharger failure due to oil starvation or carbon buildup around 80k miles.
Piezo injector failure causing misfires, rough idle, and white exhaust smoke.
Timing belt snap if not replaced every 4 years/60k miles, destroying the engine.
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K Renault Megane engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: A robust 1.9L unit that provided strong mid-range torque before being replaced by smaller, more efficient diesels.
commonFailures: Swirl flap linkage breaks, dropping plastic debris into the intake manifold.
Timing belt tensioner failure leading to catastrophic valve damage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9Q Renault Megane engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Introduced to replace the 1.9 dCi, offering better refinement and emissions compliance but suffering from EGR issues.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging from short journey driving, causing limp mode.
Timing chain stretch on early units leading to rattle and potential failure.
entries.cta: Get quotes for R9M Renault Megane engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: A direct-injection turbo petrol designed for efficiency, but infamous for severe timing chain and oil consumption flaws.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and tensioner failure causing catastrophic engine knocking.
Excessive oil consumption due to faulty piston ring design, leading to engine seizure.
entries.cta: Get quotes for H5F Renault Megane engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: A naturally aspirated workhorse known for simplicity and reliability, widely used in fleet and rental Meganes.
commonFailures: Coil pack failures causing misfires and rough running.
Thermostat housing cracks leading to coolant loss and overheating.
entries.cta: Get quotes for K4M Renault Megane engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The legendary performance heart of the Renault Sport models, highly tunable and robust when properly maintained.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 16V 135, Renault Sport 225, Renault Sport 250, Renault Sport 265
commonFailures: Timing belt failure if service intervals are exceeded, resulting in bent valves.
Coil pack and spark plug degradation causing misfires under hard acceleration.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F4R Renault Megane engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Renault Megane Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Megane’s engine reliability varies drastically by generation, with the 1.2 TCe petrol and 1.5 dCi diesel dominating replacement enquiries due to timing chain and turbo failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to understand your repair options and costs.

problems.group: Renault Megane 1.2 TCe Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Renault Megane 1.2 TCe Timing Chain Failure - Why H5F Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioner fails to maintain adequate oil pressure, causing the chain to stretch, slap, and eventually jump teeth or snap.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, and guides before failure occurs.
repairOptions.longevity: Adds 50k+ miles; only viable if no internal damage exists.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt H5F with upgraded tensioner and piston rings.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores to factory specs with 12-month warranty; best value post-failure.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattle on cold starts, act immediately. A preventative chain kit at a specialist is cost-effective, but once the chain snaps, the engine is scrap. At that point, a rebuilt unit with upgraded components is the only sensible financial choice for a Megane worth £2,000-£4,000.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt H5F engine prices for your Renault Megane

problems.group: Renault Megane 1.5 dCi Turbo & Injector Failure
problems.h4: Renault Megane 1.5 dCi Turbo & Injector Failure - K9K Diesel Weak Points
problems.rootCause: Oil starvation to the turbocharger due to clogged oil feed pipes, followed by high-pressure piezo injector wear from poor fuel quality.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo, oil feed pipes, and intercooler flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Fixes power loss; must address root oil starvation to survive.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty piezo injectors and perform software reset.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth idle; essential before turbo replacement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with uprated oil pump and new turbo.
repairOptions.longevity: The ultimate fix if metal debris from turbo has entered the sump.

problems.recommendation: Never just replace the turbo without changing the oil feed pipes and flushing the intercooler. If the turbo has disintegrated and sent metal into the engine, do not repair it-replace the entire engine with a rebuilt K9K to avoid a second catastrophic failure in six months.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K9K Renault Megane engine replacement from UK specialists

problems.group: Renault Megane 1.5 dCi Timing Belt & Valve Damage
problems.h4: Renault Megane 1.5 dCi Timing Belt & Valve Damage - The Silent Killer
problems.affectedModels: Renault Megane 1.5 dCi all variants (2001-Present, K9K family)
problems.rootCause: The cambelt snaps or jumps teeth due to age, water pump seizure, or incorrect tensioning during a previous service.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace belt, tensioner, and water pump as preventative maintenance.
repairOptions.longevity: Essential every 4 years/60k miles; prevents all downstream damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K after belt snap caused piston/valve collision.
repairOptions.longevity: Only option once the belt snaps; cylinder head is usually beyond saving.

problems.recommendation: The 1.5 dCi is an interference engine; when the belt snaps, the pistons smash into the valves. There is no repair for this damage. If your belt has snapped, cut your losses and get a quote for a rebuilt engine with a fresh timing belt kit included.
problems.cta: Find a reconditioned 1.5 dCi engine with 12-month warranty

problems.group: Renault Megane 1.6 Petrol Overheating & Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: Renault Megane 1.6 Petrol Overheating & Head Gasket Failure
problems.rootCause: The plastic thermostat housing cracks and leaks coolant, leading to overheating and subsequent head gasket blowout.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip engine, skim cylinder head, fit new gasket and housing.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if the block isn't warped; cheaper than replacement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K4M with updated metal thermostat housing.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if the cylinder head has warped beyond skimming limits.

problems.recommendation: Catch the plastic thermostat housing crack early by checking for slow coolant loss. If you see white smoke or mayonnaise in the oil, the head gasket has gone. A rebuilt K4M is often more cost-effective than a head gasket job on a high-mileage Megane.
problems.cta: Compare Renault Megane 1.6 engine prices from trusted UK rebuilders

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Megane
finalCta.paragraph: Whether it’s the notorious 1.2 TCe timing chain or a 1.5 dCi turbo failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault Megane specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components to fix the original design flaws, a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Megane Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Megane and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Renault Megane Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Renault Megane is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years, and common failure notes to help you identify your unit.

items.title: Renault Megane 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L dCi is the undisputed backbone of the Megane diesel range, appearing in every generation since 2001. Known for exceptional fuel economy, it powers the vast majority of UK Meganes but requires strict maintenance to avoid turbo and injector issues.
commonFailurePoints: timing belt snap if intervals are missed.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Megane 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Megane 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced in 2012 to replace the 1.9L, the 1.6L dCi offered better refinement and emissions compliance. It is a capable motorway cruiser but suffers from EGR clogging on short-journey cars.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging causing limp mode
early timing chain stretch on pre-2015 units.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Megane 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Megane 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L dCi was the workhorse of the Megane II and early III generations, offering strong mid-range torque. It is now largely found in high-mileage examples requiring replacement.
commonFailurePoints: Swirl flap linkage failure dropping debris into cylinders
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Megane 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Megane 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L dCi provided flagship performance for the Megane III, combining strong power with reasonable economy. It is a robust unit but expensive to replace if neglected.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Megane 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Megane 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L TCe was Renault’s attempt at a downsized turbo petrol, but it is infamous for catastrophic timing chain and oil consumption failures that dominate replacement enquiries.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch causing engine knocking
excessive oil consumption leading to seizure.
items.cta: Compare Renault Megane 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Megane 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L 16V was an entry-level naturally aspirated petrol for the early Megane III. It is simple, cheap to fix, and generally reliable if serviced correctly.
items.cta: Compare Renault Megane 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Megane 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L 16V replaced the 1.4L as the core naturally aspirated petrol. It is a robust fleet favourite, though prone to cooling system issues that can destroy the head gasket.
commonFailurePoints: Plastic thermostat housing cracks leading to coolant loss
items.cta: Compare Renault Megane 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Megane 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L 16V powered both standard models and the legendary Renault Sport variants. In RS form, it is a highly tunable performance engine; in standard form, it is a reliable cruiser.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure if intervals are exceeded
coil pack degradation under hard acceleration.
items.cta: Compare Renault Megane 2.0L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Renault Megane Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Renault Megane engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the early 1.9 dCi to the latest 1.3 TCe.

items.title: Renault Megane Diesel Engines
items.description: The diesel Megane dominates the UK used market, with the 1.5 dCi and 1.9 dCi generating the highest volume of replacement enquiries due to turbo and timing belt failures.
items.descriptor: The diesel Megane dominates the UK used market, with the 1.5 dCi and 1.9 dCi generating the highest volume of replacement enquiries due to turbo and timing belt failures.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Megane Diesel Engine Families
families: K9K (1.5 dCi) - The volume seller; prone to turbo and injector failures if oil changes are neglected.
F9Q (1.9 dCi) - The older workhorse; suffers from swirl flap and timing belt tensioner issues.
R9M (1.6 dCi) - The refined replacement; watch for EGR clogging and early timing chain stretch.
M9R (2.0 dCi) - The performance diesel; robust but expensive to rebuild if the chain fails.
knownFor: Exceptional motorway fuel economy, often exceeding 60mpg in the 1.5 dCi.
High replacement demand for the 1.5 dCi due to turbo failures around 80k miles.
Timing belt intervals must be strictly adhered to, or the engine will self-destruct.
DPF blockages are common on models used exclusively for short city journeys.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Megane Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Renault Megane Petrol Engines
items.description: The petrol Megane ranges from the bulletproof 1.6 16V to the notorious 1.2 TCe, with the Renault Sport 2.0L commanding a loyal enthusiast following and premium rebuild prices.
items.descriptor: The petrol Megane ranges from the bulletproof 1.6 16V to the notorious 1.2 TCe, with the Renault Sport 2.0L commanding a loyal enthusiast following and premium rebuild prices.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Megane Petrol Engine Families
families: H5F (1.2 TCe) - Infamous for timing chain stretch and oil consumption; avoid if possible.
K4M (1.6 16V) - Simple and reliable, but watch for plastic thermostat housing cracks.
F4R (2.0 16V / RS) - The performance legend; robust but requires strict timing belt maintenance.
H5H (1.3 TCe) - The modern replacement for the 1.2; much more reliable with timing chain fixes.
knownFor: The 1.2 TCe is widely considered one of the worst modern petrol engines due to fatal design flaws.
The 1.6 16V is a fleet favourite, offering cheap repairs and high mileage reliability.
Renault Sport models (F4R) hold their value well; owners pay premium prices for specialist rebuilds.
The 1.3 TCe (introduced 2018) successfully addressed the timing chain fears of the 1.2.
items.cta: Compare Renault Megane Petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Renault Megane Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Renault Megane production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Megane introduced Renault’s modern engine family, focusing on reliable naturally aspirated petrols and early diesels.
keyChanges: Launch of the Megane hatchback, coupé, and cabriolet.
Introduction of the K4M 1.6L and F4R 2.0L petrol engines.
Early 1.9L diesel units fitted to fleet models.
knownFor: K4M and F4R petrols are incredibly robust and high-mileage capable.
Early 1.9 diesels lack modern refinement but are mechanically simple.
Rust and electrical gremlins are more common than engine failures.
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995-2002 Renault Megane replacement engines

years.preview: Megane II: The 1.5 dCi & RS Era
years.description: The Megane II introduced the ubiquitous 1.5 dCi diesel and the legendary Renault Sport 2.0L Turbo, dominating UK sales and replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: Launch of the 1.5 dCi (K9K) and 1.2 TCe (H5F) precursors.
Introduction of the F4Rt 2.0L Turbo for the Renault Sport 225.
Shift to common-rail diesel injection across the range.
knownFor: The 1.5 dCi becomes the UK’s best-selling family car engine, but turbo failures emerge.
The 1.9 dCi suffers from swirl flap and timing belt tensioner issues.
The RS 225 is an enthusiast icon; F4R engines are highly sought after for replacements.
1.6 16V petrols are reliable but prone to coil pack and thermostat housing failures.
years.ticker: Renault Megane 1.5 dCi engine replacement • Megane II 1.9 dCi timing belt • Renault Sport 225 engine rebuild

years.preview: Megane III: The 1.2 TCe Crisis & 1.6 dCi Arrival
years.description: The Megane III introduced the controversial 1.2 TCe petrol and the refined 1.6 dCi diesel, creating a split between reliable diesels and flawed petrols.
keyChanges: Introduction of the H5F 1.2 TCe petrol, later found to have fatal timing chain flaws.
Launch of the R9M 1.6 dCi diesel to replace the 1.9L.
Renault Sport 265 replaces the 225 with an upgraded F4R engine.
knownFor: The 1.2 TCe is a disaster; timing chain stretch and oil consumption destroy thousands of engines.
The 1.5 dCi and 1.6 dCi are solid but require strict timing belt/chain maintenance.
The 1.6 16V petrol remains a safe, cheap-to-run used buy.
RS models continue to command premium rebuild prices from enthusiasts.
years.cta: Find rebuilt engines for 2008-2016 Renault Megane
years.ticker: Megane III 1.2 TCe timing chain failure • 1.5 dCi 110 engine replacement • 1.6 dCi 130 rebuild cost
enquiries: Megane III 1.2 TCe timing chain failure

years.preview: Present - Megane IV: The 1.3 TCe Fix & Blue dCi
years.description: The Megane IV attempts to redeem Renault’s petrol reputation with the reliable 1.3 TCe, while diesels evolve to meet strict Euro 6 emissions with the Blue dCi.
keyChanges: Introduction of the H5H 1.3 TCe petrol, developed with Daimler, fixing the 1.2’s chain issues.
Launch of the K9K 1.5 Blue dCi with AdBlue for Euro 6 compliance.
Launch of the high-performance RS 280/300 with the upgraded F4R engine.
knownFor: The 1.3 TCe is widely praised for fixing the 1.2’s reliability issues; a safe used buy.
The 1.5 Blue dCi adds AdBlue complexity, which can cause sensor and pump failures.
The RS 280/300 features a much more robust F4R tune with upgraded internals.
Early models may suffer from electronics and infotainment gremlins rather than engine faults.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-Present Renault Megane replacement engines

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Renault Megane was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Renault Megane specialists.

faq.h2: Renault Megane Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Renault Megane engine?
items.answer: Yes, if the car is otherwise in good condition and the replacement cost is less than the vehicle’s value plus a new car’s deposit. For a Megane worth £2,000, a £1,200 rebuilt engine with a warranty is often cheaper than monthly car finance payments.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines cost £800-£1,800 depending on the variant and fuel type.
A replacement extends the car’s life by 100,000+ miles when properly maintained.
Older Megane II models may not be worth saving if rust or suspension issues exist.
items.cta: Compare Renault Megane engine replacement costs from UK specialists

items.question: How much does a Renault Megane 1.5 dCi engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Renault Megane 1.5 dCi engine typically costs between £800 and £1,600 for the unit alone. If you require full supply and fit, expect to pay between £1,400 and £2,400 including labour, fluids, and VAT.
keyPoints: Supply-only rebuilt K9K engines range from £800 to £1,600.
Specialist fitting adds £600-£800 for labour and ECU coding.
Main dealer replacements exceed £3,500 and are rarely cost-effective.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Megane 1.5 dCi engine supply and fit

items.question: Why do Renault Megane 1.2 TCe engines fail?
items.answer: The 1.2 TCe (H5F) fails due to a faulty timing chain tensioner that causes the chain to stretch and snap, and a piston ring design that burns excessive oil, leading to engine seizure.
keyPoints: Timing chain stretch causes a distinct rattle on cold starts around 60k miles.
Excessive oil consumption starves the engine, destroying bearings and pistons.
Once the chain snaps or the engine seizes, only a full replacement is viable.
items.cta: Find a rebuilt Renault Megane 1.2 TCe engine with upgraded parts

items.question: How long does a Renault Megane 1.5 dCi engine last?
items.answer: A well-maintained 1.5 dCi (K9K) can easily exceed 200,000 miles. However, neglecting the 4-year/60,000-mile timing belt interval or oil changes will destroy the turbo and internals prematurely.
keyPoints: Timing belts must be replaced every 4 years or 60,000 miles without exception.
Annual oil changes are critical to prevent turbocharger oil starvation.
Injectors and EGR valves typically need attention between 100k-120k miles.
items.cta: Compare Renault Megane 1.5 dCi engine prices from trusted rebuilders

items.question: Can I fit a Megane 1.5 dCi 110 engine to my 90?
items.answer: Physically yes, but electronically it is highly problematic. Swapping a K9K 656 (110hp) into a car mapped for a K9K 702 (90hp) will trigger immobiliser issues, ECU mismatches, and MOT emissions failures.
keyPoints: Engine codes must match exactly to avoid ECU and immobiliser lockouts.
Emissions standards differ between generations, failing the UK MOT.
Always buy an engine with the exact same code as your original unit.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Megane engine replacement by registration

items.question: What engines are in a Renault Megane?
items.answer: The Megane uses the K9K (1.5 dCi), R9M (1.6 dCi), and M9R (2.0 dCi) diesels, alongside the H5F (1.2 TCe), H5H (1.3 TCe), K4M (1.6), and F4R (2.0/RS) petrols across its four generations.
keyPoints: Diesels: 1.5L K9K, 1.6L R9M, 1.9L F9Q, 2.0L M9R.
Petrols: 1.2L H5F, 1.3L H5H, 1.4L K4J, 1.6L K4M, 2.0L F4R.
Engine codes are critical for compatibility; check your V5C logbook.
items.cta: View the full Renault Megane engine code specification table

items.question: Do you fit Renault Megane engines?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a network of 100+ UK specialists who provide full supply and fit services. We handle the engine sourcing, delivery, and booking the installation at a vetted local garage.
keyPoints: Supply and fit packages include the engine, labour, fluids, and disposal.
All fitting garages are vetted, rated, and reviewed by real Megane owners.
You get a single point of contact for the entire job and warranty claims.
items.cta: Find Renault Megane engine supply and fit deals near me

items.question: Where is the Renault Megane engine number?
items.answer: The engine number on a Megane is typically stamped on a flat machined pad near the dipstick tube, or on the bell housing mating surface. You also need the engine code from the V5C logbook.
keyPoints: Check the V5C logbook section D.2 for the exact engine code (e.g., K9K).
Physically locate the stamp on the engine block near the transmission bell housing.
Cleaning the area with brake cleaner and a wire brush may be necessary to read it.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Megane engine replacement using your registration

items.question: Are used Renault Megane engines reliable?
items.answer: Used engines are a budget-friendly option but carry higher risk than rebuilt units. They are only recommended for older Megane II models where a £500 engine fits the car’s residual value.
keyPoints: Used engines come with minimal warranties and unknown internal wear.
Always request a compression test and service history before purchasing.
Rebuilt engines with upgraded parts are safer for Meganes you plan to keep.
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Renault Megane engine prices

items.question: What warranty comes with a Renault Megane engine?
items.answer: Every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Rebuilt and remanufactured units often offer the option to extend this to 24 months for extra peace of mind.
keyPoints: The warranty covers all internal components against manufacturing defects.
Proof of installation by a recognized garage and correct break-in procedures is required.
Warranty claims are handled directly by the supplier, not the fitting garage.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Megane engines with a 12-month warranty
